body.thing: inference router cutover: every consumer dispatches through the dimensional resolver; the (task_class, model_id, source_name) triple monolith and ModelSpec.source_name are deleted
body.intention: I intend that after the cutover no rule triple, route(), or ModelSpec.source_name survives, yet the live proxy still resolves a dispatch, the triple-only owned-local Hex edge (devstral-small-2:24b@ollama) survives into the connections stack the resolver actually reads, the coding escalation ladder still serves the design rung (no false-halt), and every default model remains reachable

body.why: The monolith smeared model<->provider reachability across BOTH ModelSpec.source_name (1:1) and the rule triples; deleting the triples without relocating the triple-only edges would silently drop reachability while a grep-proof still passed green. The proof therefore asserts the structural deletion AND the two things a grep cannot see: the surviving Hex edge and the preserved escalation ladder.

stale_flags.reason: DEFECTIVE PROOF. Two independent defects. (1) It asserted through a live InferenceDevice(), whose HealthMonitor probes real providers on a background thread, so it went green on environment luck (Hex happened to be up on this box); a concurrent agent on the same tree saw the identical assertion fail. (2) It encoded a WRONG invariant — 'the coding escalation ladder must serve the design rung' — which merely matched route()'s already-broken rung (deepseek-r1:32b, features=[], cannot build). Its green therefore HID a real shipped bug: with coding-needs-tools off, coding resolved to a tool-less reasoning model that CONFABULATED completion (claimed it wrote a file; wrote nothing).
stale_flags.note: The replacement is HERMETIC (builds the engine directly, no device) and asserts the invariant that was actually broken: a coding request must resolve to a TOOL-CAPABLE model, so a coding failure HALTS instead of silently claiming success. Its red was taken at a25de650 — the bad close commit — so its red->green proves the FIX, not the deletion. Do not cite this artifact as evidence the cutover was proven.
